title: andropogon crassus
intro: Andropogon crassus, also known as East Indian sage or Khas grass, is a type of perennial grass native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Andropogon crassus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Andropogon crassus can be prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, or infusions, and its leaves and roots can also be dried and used in various herbal preparations.
